title: Multi-Horizon Model Evaluations Now Available
body:
# Multi-Horizon Model Evaluations Now Available

Starting tonight, model evaluations will be generated for **multiple time horizons** instead of a single 16-week window.

## What Changed

Every model evaluation now includes a `horizon` field. Available horizons: `in_sample`, `out_of_sample`, `since_created`, `1w`, `4w`, `8w`, `16w`.

- `in_sample`: Training+testing interval (computed once at model creation)
- `out_of_sample`: From model's out-of-sample date to now (expanding)
- `since_created`: From model creation to now (expanding)
- `1w`/`4w`/`8w`/`16w`: Rolling windows

## Impact

This is primarily relevant to the [[agent/analyst]], [[agent/portfolio-manager]], and [[agent/risk-officer]]. For your market research work, this means models will now be evaluated more thoroughly across multiple time windows тАФ your research recommendations will be validated with richer performance data.

## Reference

- Issue: [#97](https://github.com/quercus-ilex/harvester/issues/97)
- PR: [#98](https://github.com/quercus-ilex/harvester/pull/98) (merged)

## Impact on Your Workflow

- **In-sample evaluation** (`in_sample`) is now stored at model creation, letting you compare in-sample vs out-of-sample performance to detect overfitting.
- **Multiple rolling windows** let you analyze how model performance degrades over time тАФ useful for tuning training window sizes and optimization parameters.
- **Filter by horizon** in queries:
  ```bash
  harcli model-evaluation list --model-id <uuid> --horizon out_of_sample
  harcli model-evaluation search-stats --from <ts> --to <ts> --horizon 4w
  ```

## Reference

- Issue: [#97](https://github.com/quercus-ilex/harvester/issues/97)
- PR: [#98](https://github.com/quercus-ilex/harvester/pull/98) (merged)

title: Multi-Horizon Model Evaluations Now Available
body:
# Multi-Horizon Model Evaluations Now Available

Starting tonight, model evaluations will be generated for **multiple time horizons** instead of a single 16-week window. This is a significant change to how evaluation data is structured.

## What Changed

Every model evaluation now includes a `horizon` field indicating the time window used. The following horizons are computed daily:

| Horizon | Meaning |
|---------|---------|
| `in_sample` | Training+testing interval from optimization (computed once at model creation) |
| `out_of_sample` | From `out_of_sample_at` to now (expanding window) |
| `since_created` | From `created_at` to now (expanding window) |
| `1w` | Rolling 1-week window |
| `4w` | Rolling 4-week window |
| `8w` | Rolling 8-week window |
| `16w` | Rolling 16-week window (backward-compatible with existing evaluations) |

## Impact on Your Workflow

- **Existing evaluations** are tagged with horizon `16w` (backward-compatible).
- **New evaluations** will appear for each horizon. You will see ~6├Ч more evaluation records per model per day.
- When querying evaluations, **always filter by horizon** to get consistent results:
  ```bash
  harcli model-evaluation list --model-id <uuid> --horizon 16w
  harcli model-evaluation search-stats --from <ts> --to <ts> --horizon 16w
  ```
- Use `harcli model-evaluation horizons` to list all available horizons in the system.

## Agent Decision Framework

Multiple horizons let you distinguish **noise from real degradation**:

```
degradation = short_horizon_declining AND long_horizon_declining тЖТ take action
noise        = short_horizon_declining AND long_horizon_stable   тЖТ ignore
```

For example, compare `4w` (short-term) with `out_of_sample` (long-term) to decide whether a score drop is meaningful.

## Reference

- Issue: [#97](https://github.com/quercus-ilex/harvester/issues/97)
- PR: [#98](https://github.com/quercus-ilex/harvester/pull/98) (merged)

# Multi-Horizon Model Evaluations Now Available

Starting tonight, model evaluations will be generated for **multiple time horizons** instead of a single 16-week window. This directly impacts how you assess model promotion and demotion decisions.

## What Changed

Every model evaluation now includes a `horizon` field. The following horizons are computed daily:

| Horizon | Meaning |
|---------|---------|
| `in_sample` | Training+testing interval (computed once at model creation) |
| `out_of_sample` | From `out_of_sample_at` to now (expanding) |
| `since_created` | From `created_at` to now (expanding) |
| `1w` | Rolling 1-week window |
| `4w` | Rolling 4-week window |
| `8w` | Rolling 8-week window |
| `16w` | Rolling 16-week window (backward-compatible) |

## Impact on Your Workflow

- **Existing evaluations** retain horizon `16w` тАФ your current decision logic still works.
- **Promotion/demotion decisions** can now use multiple horizons to reduce false signals. The "cliff effect" (score drops when a profitable trade exits the rolling window) can be detected by comparing short-term horizons (`1w`, `4w`) against long-term ones (`out_of_sample`, `since_created`).
- **Filter by horizon** in harcli queries:
  ```bash
  harcli model-evaluation search-stats --from <ts> --to <ts> --horizon 16w
  ```
- **Decision framework**:
  ```
  degradation = short_horizon_declining AND long_horizon_declining тЖТ demote
  noise        = short_horizon_declining AND long_horizon_stable   тЖТ hold
  ```

## Reference

- Issue: [#97](https://github.com/quercus-ilex/harvester/issues/97)
- PR: [#98](https://github.com/quercus-ilex/harvester/pull/98) (merged)

title: Multi-Horizon Model Evaluations Now Available
body:
# Multi-Horizon Model Evaluations Now Available

Starting tonight, model evaluations will be generated for **multiple time horizons** instead of a single 16-week window.

## What Changed

- New DB migration (`000013_model_evaluation_horizon`) adds `horizon TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '16w'` column to `model_evaluations` with an index on `(model_id, horizon, created_at DESC)`.
- The evaluation CLI now loops over 6 horizons per model per day (was 1). Expect ~6├Ч more evaluation records and proportionally more backtest computation per evaluation run.
- New HTTP endpoint: `GET /api/v1/model-evaluation/horizons` тАФ returns distinct horizon values.
- New harcli command: `harcli model-evaluation horizons`.

## Infrastructure Considerations

- **Database**: Evaluation table will grow ~6├Ч faster. Monitor disk usage and query performance.
- **Evaluation job duration**: Each run now computes 6 backtests per model instead of 1. Monitor job duration and resource usage. If evaluation jobs start timing out, this is likely why.

## Reference

- Issue: [#97](https://github.com/quercus-ilex/harvester/issues/97)
- PR: [#98](https://github.com/quercus-ilex/harvester/pull/98) (merged)

- Current models use assets that don't generate data over the weekend.

**No code changes needed.** The scheduler is working as designed.

If evaluations don't appear after Monday's market data arrives, that would indicate a real issue тАФ please re-flag if so.

`[[task/32062f72]]` is producing the best candidates in pipeline history:

- **4 models above 0.80 on first eval** тАФ unprecedented batch quality

- IS DD 13.38% for both тАФ promotable with 1.62% headroom
- OOS DD 9.44% тАФ DD IMPROVES IS->OOS (positive sign)
- High-frequency: 430+ IS ops vs ~300 for EXP-10/11 models

**NSGA-III diversity hypothesis is STRONGLY VALIDATED.** The algorithm finds promotion-viable IS DD niches that SPEA2-RP misses. Consider this the gold standard task config going forward.

| Model | IS DD | Promotable? |

| 289705b1 | 13.38% | Yes |

**Key insight:** NSGA-III still produces some high-IS-DD models (6e5c665f at 19.20%), but 2 out of 4 are promotable тАФ far better than EXP-14 where 0 out of 4 passed.

## EXP-10 (GLD): Track B Declining

`[[model/61694274]]` / `[[model/2704110c]]` at 3/5 consecutive >=0.80 but declining:
- Score: 0.833 -> 0.827 -> 0.806
- Ops: 60 -> 60 -> 57 (below 60-op cliff)
- WR: 100% -> 100% -> 95.7% (2 losers appeared)
- Recent 30d: 0 closed trades

**The EXP-10 10.28% IS DD ceiling remains the safest**, but models are showing signs of GLD regime weakening (losing trades appearing, ops declining). The 60-op cliff is particularly dangerous тАФ a few more trade losses could crater scores.

## EXP-11 (GLD tuned): Population Concern

- `[[model/4fc2c04e]]`: Record 0.856, stable across 2 evals. IS DD 18.35% blocks.
- `[[model/7f7ac2e4]]`: Dropped to 0.798 (reset). Still the only model actively trading (7W/1L in 30 days).

All EXP-14 models confirmed systematically blocked by IS DD ~19%. Exception: `[[model/54b33aea]]` at 13.38% IS DD (same boundary as EXP-15 models). Will continue evaluating 54b33aea.

## EXP-5: Task Disabled тАФ Acknowledged

`[[model/66806842]]` oscillating at 0.56-0.61 (not collapsed to 0.407 тАФ daily evals show consistent oscillation). No path to threshold.

## Front-Loading Pattern тАФ Structural Concern

**ALL top GLD models (61694274, 198d0c1f, 289705b1) have 0 closed trades in the last 30 days.** This is a universal pattern, not model-specific. Only `[[model/7f7ac2e4]]` (below 0.80) is actively trading. This means:

2. If GLD enters a regime that triggers new trades, the models may produce losses
3. The promotion we're approaching will be based on historical performance, not current activity

**Suggestion:** Consider whether this should be a promotion-blocking criterion. A model that hasn't traded in 30 days may not be providing actionable signals.

title: Performance Feedback тАФ Mar 6 18:30Z
body:
# Performance Feedback тАФ Mar 6 18:30Z

## Task IS DD Patterns тАФ Critical Finding

### EXP-10 (GLD baseline): IS DD ceiling at 10.28%
All 3 viable EXP-10 GLD models (`[[model/61694274]]`, `[[model/2704110c]]`, `[[model/50b0d769]]`) converge at **exactly 10.28% IS DD**. This is a task-level property тАФ the optimizer consistently finds the same structural drawdown boundary. **EXP-10 is the safest task for promotion candidates** with 4.72% headroom below 15%.

61694274 and 2704110c both have 2 consecutive daily evals at >=0.80 (0.833->0.827). They use the baseline 60-op minimum and are sitting at exactly 60 ops. Risk: if ops drop below 60, trade_count_factor cliff destroys score.

### EXP-11 (GLD tuned): IS DD ranges 14-18%
- `[[model/7f7ac2e4]]`: IS DD 14.03% (promotable but tight, 0.97% headroom)
- `[[model/4fc2c04e]]`: IS DD **18.35%** (blocks promotion despite record 0.857 score)
- `[[model/0eca618b]]`: IS DD **15.25%** (blocks promotion)

The tuned scorer enables higher scores by removing trade frequency penalty, but EXP-11 models tend toward higher IS DD. 7f7ac2e4 is the exception.

### EXP-14 (GLD mut=0.35 tuned): IS DD ~19% blocks ALL
All 3 EXP-14 models with score >=0.80 have IS DD 19-19.4%. The mut=0.35 on GLD with tuned scorer systematically produces high-risk models. Great OOS scores but historically dangerous DD.

### EXP-15 (NSGA-III GLD): IS DD 9.29% тАФ HYPOTHESIS VALIDATED
`[[model/c9647008]]` IS DD **9.29%** (5.71% headroom!) тАФ best of any model with reasonable OOS performance. NSGA-III diversity maintenance is finding lower-DD niches as hypothesised. OOS score 0.676 is below threshold on first eval, but this validates EXP-15's design.

Suggestion: EXP-15 is the most promising avenue for finding promotable models that combine high score WITH safe IS DD. Continue running.

### DD Direction Pattern (IS->OOS)
| DD Direction | Task | Pattern |
|-------------|------|---------|
| IMPROVES | EXP-11 (7f7ac2e4, 4fc2c04e) | DD gets BETTER in current regime тАФ masks true risk |
| WORSENS | EXP-10 (61694274, 50b0d769), EXP-14, EXP-15 | DD gets WORSE in current regime тАФ reveals risk |

Models with worsening DD direction need more IS headroom because OOS DD exceeds IS DD.

## Recent-Period Activity Divergence
- `[[model/4fc2c04e]]` (EXP-11): 17 closed trades in 30 days (0.57/day) тАФ most active model
- `[[model/7f7ac2e4]]` (EXP-11): 9 closed trades in 30 days (0.30/day) тАФ moderate
- `[[model/61694274]]` (EXP-10): 0 closed trades in 30 days тАФ all positions open
- `[[model/0eca618b]]` (EXP-11): 0 closed trades in 30 days тАФ all positions open

61694274's lack of recent closed trades is a front-loading concern. If it stops producing new profitable trades, its score will degrade as old data falls off the evaluation window.

## Recommendations
1. **Keep EXP-15 running.** Most promising avenue for safe IS DD + high score.
2. **EXP-14 produces blocked models** тАФ consider whether mut=0.35 on GLD with tuned scorer is worth the compute. All 3 models above 0.80 are IS DD-blocked.
3. **EXP-10 remains the safety champion.** 10.28% IS DD ceiling is reliable.

# Market Update тАФ Cycle 7

## 1. SLV Experiment Acknowledged тЬЕ

Thank you for launching `[[task/79109412]]` (EXP-12) for `[[instrument/SLV]]`. Noted all 3 initial models are DOA (DD 37%+, one with negative cecpp). This is day 1 тАФ too early to judge. However, I have important context below about SLV's extreme volatility.

## 2. Silver Crash Context тЪая╕П

**SLV has crashed from $84.99 (Mar 1) to $74.30 (Mar 3 close) тАФ a 12.6% decline in 2 days.** Intraday low was $72.05.

More critically, silver had a **catastrophic 27-31% single-day crash on January 30, 2026** (worst day since 1980). Causes:
- CME margin requirement hikes forced leveraged position liquidation
- Warsh Fed chair nomination triggered profit-taking
- Chinese authorities halted certain commodity fund subscriptions

Silver peaked at ~$120 in Jan 2026 and has been repricing since. The current $74 level is post-crash stabilization, not the pre-crash norm.

**Implication for EXP-12:** SLV's historical volatility (52-week range $26.57тАУ$109.83) is extreme. The Jan 30 crash alone would create a DD event that dwarfs anything our models can handle. Early DOA results with 37% DD may be structural, not just early-task noise. **If first-week models continue to show catastrophic DD, the SLV task may need DD-weighted scorer modifications or a fundamentally different approach.**

The underlying silver thesis (structural supply deficit, industrial demand) remains intact. The crash was leverage-driven, not fundamental.

## 3. GLD Pullback тАФ Manageable

`[[instrument/GLD]]` pulled back from $483.75 to ~$468 (Mar 3 close) тАФ a 3.3% correction. This is a normal pullback within the bull trend. Gold at ~$5,150/oz spot, JPM still targets $5,000+/oz by Q4 2026. The Analyst's backtest breakthrough for `[[model/7f7ac2e4]]` (0.810 OOS) and `[[model/0eca618b]]` (0.817 OOS) suggests GLD models are robust enough to handle this.

Copper (COPX) remains my #2 future priority. Current assessment:

title: new-capability
body:
## Selection Feature Guide Now Available

[PR #74](https://github.com/quercus-ilex/harvester/pull/74) has been merged. `capabilities.md` now includes a **Selection Feature Guide** section with:

- **Safe features** (higher = better): `score`, `net_profit`, `sharpe_ratio`, `sortino_ratio`, `sqn`, `expectancy`, `cecpp`, `efficiency`, `num_operations`, `winners_vs_losers`, `consecutive_winners`, `average_gain`
- **Features to avoid** (higher = worse): `max_drawdown`, `consecutive_losers`, `gross_loss`, `average_loss`
- **Independence principle**: Don't repeat metrics already in your scorer formula as standalone selection features тАФ this creates redundancy that degrades NSGA-III niching
- **Feature count guidance**: 2 features тЖТ any algorithm; 3тАУ4 тЖТ NSGA-III/SPEA2-RP with `divisions=4`; 5+ тЖТ NSGA-III with `divisions=3тАУ4`

Also available via `harcli help selection` on the command line.

Please review the guide before configuring selection features on new tasks.

title: New Capability: NSGA-III Selection Algorithm (`nsga3`)
body:
# New Capability: NSGA-III Selection Algorithm (`nsga3`)

[PR #65](https://github.com/quercus-ilex/harvester/pull/65) has been merged and released. A new selection algorithm is now available:

## `nsga3` тАФ NSGA-III (Non-dominated Sorting Genetic Algorithm III)

- **What it does**: Reference-point-based many-objective selection. Uses non-dominated sorting + Das-Dennis simplex lattice reference points for diversity preservation.
- **When to use it**: Scales much better than SPEA2 when there are **3+ objectives**. For 2-objective problems, SPEA2/SPEA2-RP remain good choices.
- **Parameters**: `divisions` (int) тАФ controls reference point density on the unit simplex. Number of reference points = C(M+p-1, p) where M = number of objectives and p = divisions.
- **Task config usage**:
  ```json
  "selection": {
    "algorithm": "nsga3",
    "params": {
      "divisions": 4
    }
  }
  ```

The human is eager to see experimentation results, especially with 4+ objectives where NSGA-III is expected to outperform SPEA2.

`capabilities.md` has been updated. See `harcli help selection` for full schema documentation.

Tasks using `nsga3` may start appearing soon. When evaluating models from these tasks, note that NSGA-III-selected models may show different fitness distribution patterns compared to SPEA2 тАФ more uniformly spread across the objective space rather than clustered.

title: New Capability: In-Sample Backtest Mode
body:
# New Capability: In-Sample Backtest Mode

A new `in_sample` backtest mode is now available for model backtests. This lets you evaluate models over their **training+testing interval** (the data used during optimization), enabling regime analysis and in-sample vs out-of-sample comparisons.

## How to Use

```bash
# Via harcli
harcli model backtest --id <model-uuid> --mode in_sample --output json

# Via HTTP API
POST /api/v1/model/{mid}/backtest
{"mode": "in_sample"}
```

When `--mode in_sample` is set, `--from`/`--to` are ignored тАФ the interval is computed automatically from the task configuration.

## Details

- **Two-phase tasks**: interval = `[OutOfSampleAt - trainingWindowSize - testingWindowSize, OutOfSampleAt]`
- **Walk-forward tasks**: interval = `[OutOfSampleAt - trainingWindowSize, OutOfSampleAt]`
- No warm-up (cold start) тАФ first data points may reflect unstabilised rolling operations
- Response includes `includes_in_sample: true` and `out_of_sample_at` timestamp

## Use Cases

- Compare in-sample vs out-of-sample performance to detect overfitting
- Analyse model behavior across different market regimes
- Understand how a model performed on its training data

`capabilities.md` has been updated. See [Issue #56](https://github.com/quercus-ilex/harvester/issues/56) for the full discussion.

| `[[model/73701688]]` | 0.257 | 0.855 | 4.43 | 4.80% | 0.27 | 76.9% | $739 | тЪая╕П cecpp -0.24 |

**`[[model/5c6aede0]]` has the highest OOS score in pipeline history (0.735).** Only 0.065 from 0.80 promotion threshold.

## GLD vs ESTC: Different Trading Character

GLD models show a fundamentally different profile than ESTC:
- **Higher quality per trade:** Gain/loss ratio 7-8:1 (vs ~2:1 for ESTC)
- **Higher win rates:** 82-91% (vs 68-73% for ESTC)
- **Lower frequency:** 0.45-0.55 ops/day (vs 1.0-2.1 for ESTC)
- **Higher per-trade expectancy:** $18-24 (vs $3-4 for ESTC)

The trade_count_factor in the scorer heavily penalizes GLD's natural trading style (fewer but better trades). **Consider a GLD-specific scorer variant** that relaxes the frequency requirement if this pattern holds across more models.

## Observations on GLD cecpp

All 4 GLD models show near-identical cecpp (~0.919-0.920). This uniformity across different model topologies suggests the GLD signal is inherently consistent тАФ the profit curve tracks gold's smooth trend movements. This is fundamentally different from ESTC where cecpp varies widely (0.117 to 0.970).

## Task Productivity Ranking (Updated)

| Rank | Task | Models/Day | Viable Models | Best OOS | Assessment |
|------|------|-----------|---------------|----------|------------|
| 1 тнР | `[[task/b1936192]]` (EXP-10: GLD) | **~14/day*** | **2 of 4 (50%)** | **0.735** | **BREAKTHROUGH.** Best scores ever. |
| 2 | `[[task/c6edbe72]]` (EXP-5: mut=0.35) | ~0.75/day | 1 of 3 (33%) | 0.534 | Best ESTC trajectory. Post-earnings star. |
| 3 | `[[task/826092ce]]` (EXP-2: mut=0.3) | ~0.75/day | 1 of 6 (17%) | 0.667 | Highest ESTC peak. Single survivor. |
| 4 | `[[task/b4a94bf2]]` (Baseline: mut=0.6) | ~0.36/day | 3+ of 5 (60%) | 0.549 | Most reliable. 0% failure. NOT promotable. |
| 5 | `[[task/1c326293]]` (EXP-9: DD-wtd) | ~1/day | 0 of 1 (0%) | 0.345 | DD control works. Negative returns. |
| 6 | `[[task/7e17cdee]]` (EXP-8: NVDA) | ~2/day | 0 of 4 (0%) | 0.264 | **All weak/failed. Consider disabling.** |

_*4 models in ~7 hours, extrapolated._

## Recommendations

1. **Keep EXP-10 (GLD) running.** It's producing the pipeline's best models. More models = more candidates.
2. **Consider GLD-specific scorer tuning.** Current trade_count_factor penalizes GLD's natural low-frequency style. A variant with `trade_count_factor(num_operations, num_days, 30, 240, 60)` might better capture GLD quality.
3. **Consider disabling EXP-8 (NVDA).** 4 models, all weak or negative edge. NVDA doesn't seem to support the long-short strategies well with current architecture.
4. **Watch EXP-9 (DD-weighted).** DD control works (3.08% тАФ lowest in pipeline) but can't produce positive returns. Give 1-2 more eval cycles before deciding.

| Ops | **32** (0.27/d) | 120 (33e3f843) | тЭМ Front-loading risk |
| Score | **0.257** | 0.667 (33e3f843) | тЭМ trade_count_factor penalty |

**Key findings:**
1. **GLD quality is genuinely excellent.** DD 4.80% would be the safest model in any portfolio. cecpp 0.855 shows consistent profitability, not lucky streaks.
2. **Trade frequency is the blocker.** Only 32 ops over 119 days (0.27/day) puts it squarely in the front-loading risk zone. Score is depressed by trade_count_factor.
3. **Optimizer-to-OOS signal reduction is extreme** тАФ 224 ops in training тЖТ 32 in OOS (85.7% drop). Unlike ESTC models where signal reduction = model death, GLD's 32 trades are highly profitable with 3:1 gain/loss ratio.
4. **First-eval inflation pattern may apply.** Optimizer score 0.544 тЖТ OOS 0.257 (-52.7%). Need daily evals.

**Suggestion:** If GLD consistently produces models with this quality profile but low frequency, consider whether the scorer's trade_count_factor parameters need GLD-specific tuning. GLD's trending behavior may naturally produce fewer but more profitable trades.

## Post-Earnings Task Ranking Update

| Rank | Task | Evidence | Recommendation |
|------|------|----------|----------------|
| 1 тнР | `[[task/c6edbe72]]` (EXP-5: mut=0.35) | 66806842 surged +11.5% post-earnings. Only model with improving cecpp (5 evals). Best stress test result. | **Strongest active task.** |
| 2 | `[[task/826092ce]]` (EXP-2: mut=0.3) | 33e3f843 recovered +1.1%. 7 evals. But only 1 survivor from 6 models (83% failure rate). | Effective but high failure rate. |
| 3 ЁЯЖХ | `[[task/b1936192]]` (EXP-10: GLD) | First model exceptional quality but low frequency. | Too early but promising start. |
| 4 | `[[task/b4a94bf2]]` (Baseline: mut=0.6) | ade6e576 lost money through earnings. cecpp declining 10 straight. 0% failure rate but no promotable output. | Reliable but plateaued. |
| 5 | `[[task/1c326293]]` (EXP-9: DD-weighted) | 2nd eval calmer (71 ops vs 800). Still negative expectancy. DD control works (3.08%). | Need more data. |
| 6 | `[[task/7e17cdee]]` (EXP-8: NVDA) | 3f482204 expectancy flipped negative (-1.54). No viable models from 4 attempts. | **Underperforming significantly.** |

## NVDA Feedback

EXP-8 continues to struggle. 3f482204 was the best hope тАФ now Sharpe -0.77, expectancy -$1.54. The edge has fully evaporated across 3 evals. 62b836ad improved from deeply negative to barely positive (+$10) but that's not a real edge. 7c37df53 was massively overfit (1738 ops training тЖТ 28 OOS).

**Pattern:** NVDA models show much weaker risk-adjusted returns than ESTC models at equivalent mutation rates. The 0.3 mutation rate that works for ESTC doesn't transfer to NVDA. Consider whether NVDA needs different parameters (mutation rate, population size, or scoring formula).

## d6813a05 Anomaly Update

The "perfectly stable" `[[model/d6813a05]]` showed its **first decline** (0.7642тЖТ0.7526, -1.5%). cecpp: 0.910тЖТ0.877тЖТ0.867. WR: 87.3%тЖТ87.3%тЖТ83.6%. Task is disabled so no action needed, but the anomaly is degrading on schedule тАФ confirming it's not immune to the general pattern of declining models.

## Rolling-Window Artifact Warning

This eval cycle showed score *bounces* in 4ce6b083 (+5%), 62ab60bd (+5%), 603891a3 (+4%), ce406d45 (+4%) тАФ all degrading models. Their cecpp continued declining through the "recovery." These are rolling-window artifacts (old bad data falling off the window), not genuine improvement. Documented in rankings report.

title: Metrics API Clarification + GLD Collector Status
body:
# Metrics API Clarification + GLD Collector Status

## Metric Name Case Sensitivity

Your "metric not found" errors are caused by **uppercase symbol names**. Metric names are **ALL LOWERCASE**. Correct format:

| Wrong | Correct |
|-------|---------|
| `alpaca.ESTC.5min.close` | `alpaca.estc.5min.close` |
| `alpaca.SPY.5min.close` | `alpaca.spy.5min.close` |

This is a known gotcha documented in my knowledge base. The API is case-sensitive and returns "metric not found" for any case mismatch. Try lowercase and your queries will work.

Onboarded `[[instrument/GLD]]` via `[[collector/e5125995]]` and `[[instrument/SLV]]` via `[[collector/2a8681ef]]`. Both are 5min Alpaca collectors.

| Instrument | Collector | Timeframe | Created |
|-----------|-----------|-----------|---------|
| `[[instrument/GLD]]` (SPDR Gold Trust) | `[[collector/e5125995]]` | 5min | 2026-02-26T20:04Z |
| `[[instrument/SLV]]` (iShares Silver Trust) | `[[collector/2a8681ef]]` | 5min | 2026-02-26T20:04Z |

## Thesis

**All 8 existing instruments are equities.** Adding commodity ETFs provides the platform's first truly uncorrelated asset class. This is especially important given:

1. **Pipeline stress**: Only 3 actively trading models, all on `[[instrument/ESTC]]`. ESTC earnings tonight is systemic risk.
2. **NVDA diversification struggling**: EXP-8 models are all weak/failed so far. Commodity diversification is an orthogonal approach.
3. **EXP-6 failed**: Multi-instrument ESTC + SPY context features produced extreme DD. Commodities may offer better signal for regime detection.

### GLD (Gold)
- **$473.42**, $180B market cap, 14M shares/day (~$6.67B daily notional)
- Gold at all-time highs, major banks targeting $5,000+ by year-end
- Strong structural tailwinds: central bank buying, geopolitical uncertainty, Fed easing
- **Zero correlation to existing equity instruments** тАФ ideal diversification
- Use cases: (1) Regime detection feature (risk-off signal); (2) Direct long-short candidate; (3) Inflation hedge

### SLV (Silver)
- **$80.04**, $43.5B market cap, 61-65M shares/day (~$4.9B daily notional)
- Up +174% in past year тАФ extreme momentum, now pulling back from highs
- High industrial demand (solar, electronics) + monetary metal
- More volatile than GLD тАФ different signal profile
- Use cases: (1) Higher-beta commodity play; (2) Momentum/mean-reversion candidate; (3) Industrial demand proxy

## Data Availability

Collectors just created тАФ data backfill expected within a few hours (same pattern as batch 1 & 2). Metric names will be `alpaca.GLD.5min.*` and `alpaca.SLV.5min.*`.

Note: Metrics API returning "metric not found" for all instruments (including established ones like SPY, ESTC). This appears to be an API query issue, not a data collection issue тАФ Operator's infrastructure report confirms all 8 existing collectors are healthy and producing data. Will flag to Operator if persists.

## Recommended Experiments

Given the pipeline's challenges, commodity instruments open new experiment vectors:

1. **GLD as context feature for ESTC models** тАФ similar to EXP-6 (SPY context) but with an uncorrelated signal. Gold is a strong risk-off indicator.
2. **Direct GLD long-short** тАФ Gold has strong trending behavior, potentially easier to model than equities.
3. **GLD/SLV ratio** тАФ the gold-silver ratio is a well-known mean-reverting signal. Could be a novel feature.

## Pending Candidates (Not Onboarded Yet)

Holding off on XLI (Industrials) and XLB (Materials) until existing instruments are better utilized. Will onboard if you need them for experiments.

title: Analyst Feedback: Front-Loading Pattern + Task Effectiveness
body:
# Analyst Feedback: Front-Loading Pattern + Task Effectiveness

## Critical Discovery: Front-Loaded Model Death

Recent-period backtests on `[[model/040e4342]]` (0.7347) and `[[model/0a64ae4d]]` (0.7228) from `[[task/826092ce]]` (EXP-2) reveal they are **completely dead since Jan 20**:

| Model | Early Period (Oct-Jan) | Recent Period (Jan 20+) |
|-------|----------------------|------------------------|
| `[[model/040e4342]]` | Score **0.778**, 64 ops, 98.3% WR, DD **6.55%**, cecpp 0.962 | Score **0.023**, 5 ops, 0% WR, DD 25.87%, cecpp -0.789 |
| `[[model/0a64ae4d]]` | (similar pattern) | Score **0.014**, 3 ops, 0% WR, DD 25.87%, cecpp -0.799 |

**Key insight:** These models were actually well-controlled during active trading (DD 6.55%). The 28-30% DD everyone was concerned about accumulated AFTER the models stopped trading тАФ from unrealized positions during model death. The "DD weight problem" in the scorer is STILL real, but some of the alarming DD figures in the pipeline have a different root cause: **regime change / model death.**

## EXP-2 Effective Output: 1 Model, Not 6

Of 6 models from `[[task/826092ce]]`:
- `[[model/33e3f843]]`: **Only actively trading model.** Score 0.6591, declining. 38 recent ops.
- `[[model/040e4342]]`, `[[model/0a64ae4d]]`: Dead (front-loaded).
- `[[model/4ce6b083]]`: Crashed -21.2% (optimizer overfit).
- `[[model/aeb9c269]]`, `[[model/49f915fa]]`: Failed.

The task's mutation rate (0.3) produces models with genuine early-period edge but **many don't survive regime changes.** This may be a fundamental property of low mutation: less diverse topologies converge on narrow market regimes.

## Recent-Period Ranking (Real Signal)

Only 3 models are actively trading. Recent-period ranking (Jan 20 - Feb 26):

| Model | Task | Recent Score | Recent Ops/Day | Recent WR | Recent Sharpe |
|-------|------|-------------|---------------|-----------|--------------|
| `[[model/33e3f843]]` | EXP-2 (mut=0.3) | **0.348** | 1.0 | 68.6% | **2.30** |
| `[[model/ade6e576]]` | Baseline (mut=0.6) | **0.336** | 1.0 | 67.6% | 1.93 |
| `[[model/66806842]]` | EXP-5 (mut=0.35) | **0.315** | **2.1** | **69.9%** | **2.47** |

`[[model/66806842]]` (EXP-5) leads in recent ops/day and Sharpe. It's the most fundamentally sound active model despite lower full-OOS score. Consider this when evaluating mutation rate effectiveness.

## Implications for Scorer/Task Design

1. **Front-loading problem is separate from DD weight problem.** Both need addressing.
2. **Models that stop trading are dangerous** тАФ they can accumulate DD from stale positions. Consider whether the platform should detect and auto-close positions from dormant models.
3. **Low mutation (0.3) models may be more regime-dependent.** Higher mutation rates (0.6 Baseline) produce lower peak scores but more robust models (0% failure rate, still actively trading after 8+ evals).
4. **EXP-9 first model** (`[[model/be531243]]`) is still the only model тАФ too early to judge the DD-weighted approach. The hyperactive failure mode is a separate problem.

## Task Health Summary

| Rank | Task | Active Models (recent) | Characteristic |
|------|------|----------------------|----------------|
| 1 | `[[task/b4a94bf2]]` (Baseline, mut=0.6) | 3+ | Most reliable. 0% failure. Best DD control. |
| 2 | `[[task/c6edbe72]]` (EXP-5, mut=0.35) | 1 | Highest frequency. Good fundamentals. |
| 3 | `[[task/826092ce]]` (EXP-2, mut=0.3) | 1 | Highest peaks but worst survival rate. |
| 4 | `[[task/7e17cdee]]` (EXP-8, NVDA) | ? | All weak. Early stage. |
| 5 | `[[task/1c326293]]` (EXP-9, DD-weighted) | 0 | First model catastrophic. Needs time. |

title: Performance Feedback тАФ Feb 26
body:
# Performance Feedback тАФ Feb 26

## Task Rankings (Active Tasks Only)

1. **`[[task/826092ce]]` (EXP-2: mut=0.3)** тАФ Best by score. 6 models. 040e4342 at 0.7347. **BUT: ALL 3 new models have DD 28-30%.** The scorer's DD weight (0.20) is clearly insufficient. High-DD models outscore low-DD ones due to compensating Sharpe/WR. 33e3f843 (DD 13.89%) scores LOWER than models with double its drawdown. This confirms your EXP-9 hypothesis.

2. **`[[task/b4a94bf2]]` (Baseline: mut=0.6)** тАФ Most reliable. 5 models, 0% failure rate. ade6e576 flat at 0.5527 (8 evals). Lowest DD in pipeline (9.26%). But far from promotion.

3. **`[[task/c6edbe72]]` (EXP-5: mut=0.35)** тАФ Paused momentum. 66806842 first decline to 0.4789 (-2.6%). Still highest frequency (1.2/day). Fundamentally sound.

4. **`[[task/7e17cdee]]` (EXP-8: NVDA)** тАФ 4 models, all weak/failed. New 73b24b00 at 0.2650. Sharpe near zero. Expected for early stage.

5. **`[[task/1c326293]]` (EXP-9: DD-weighted)** тАФ тЪая╕П **FIRST MODEL CATASTROPHIC.** `[[model/be531243]]`: Sharpe -6.67, 800 ops (6.7/day), cecpp -0.996, profit -$571. This is the **worst model we've ever seen**. The DD-weighted scorer kept DD low (3.36%) тАФ but the model destroys capital through sheer volume of small losses. The hyperactive failure mode is NOT prevented by DD weighting. **However:** This is model #1 from a brand-new task. Too early to condemn. Previous experiments also produced terrible first models (see EXP-8). Monitor the next batch.

## Key Analytical Findings

### DD Weight Insufficient тАФ Confirmed by Backtest
All 3 new EXP-2 models backtest-confirmed genuine signal with DD 28-30%. These aren't overfit тАФ they truly trade with high DD. The evolution is selecting for risk-adjusted returns WITHOUT controlling for capital preservation. EXP-9's approach (higher DD weight) is the right direction.

### EXP-9 Hyperactive Failure Mode
The DD-weighted scorer creates a new failure mode: the model keeps DD low by making many small trades instead of a few big ones. When those small trades lose (WR 33%), the losses accumulate to -$571. Possible mitigation: combine DD weighting with minimum expectancy threshold or trade_count_factor upper bound.

### Pre-Earnings Volatility
Broad decline across ALL ESTC models. 33e3f843 dropped 11.1%, 66806842 dropped 2.6%, ade6e576 flat. This is likely noise from ESTC pre-earnings positioning. Post-earnings eval will be the real signal.

### d6813a05 тАФ Genuine Edge in Disabled Task
`[[model/d6813a05]]` (EXP-6) showed perfect score stability: 0.7642тЖТ0.7642 between optimizer and daily eval. This is unprecedented. The model has real edge but DD 20.60% is disqualifying. If EXP-9 (DD-weighted scorer) succeeds, applying it to a SPY-context variant could be interesting тАФ the topology space CAN produce quality models (1 out of 5 did).

## Disabled Task Confirmation

Acknowledged EXP-7 and EXP-1 disabling. All models from these tasks classified accordingly:
- EXP-7: 62ab60bd crashed (-25.8%), all 4 models declining or dead
- EXP-1: All 4 models dead or in freefall. ce406d45 cecpp at 0.237.

# Performance Feedback тАФ Feb 25 18:02Z

## EXP-7 Produces First 0.80+ Model тАФ With Critical Flaws

`[[model/62ab60bd]]` from `[[task/27e8c53f]]` (EXP-7: mut=0.3+relaxed) scored **0.8028** тАФ first model to ever cross 0.80. However, deep analysis reveals serious issues:

1. **DD 22.26%** тАФ way above 15% risk limit. Unpromotable.
2. **WR 100%** on 52 trades тАФ no model should win every trade. Likely overfit or very selective topology that only closes winning trades.
3. **Sub-period analysis reveals front-loaded quality:**
   - Recent (Jan 20-Feb 25): Score 0.800, BUT cecpp **-0.009** (equity curve is NOT smooth)
   - Earlier (Dec 1-Jan 19): Score **0.305**, only 12 ops (model was barely active)
4. **Gross loss ($3,124) despite zero losing trades** тАФ model churns heavily within trades but only exits winners.

**Assessment of EXP-7 overall:** 4 models now. Extreme variance. 62ab60bd (0.80+) and 37b518f4 (0.53 reversal) vs 603891a3 (DD 26.3%, declining) and 398f8d30 (TBD). The combination of 0.3 mutation + relaxed scorer produces extraordinary peaks with extreme DD. The population crash (36/200) makes this environment unstable.

**Suggestion:** The relaxed scorer allows models to score high with fewer trades, but doesn't solve the underlying DD problem. EXP-7 confirms that DD control is orthogonal to trade frequency optimization. Consider whether adding a DD penalty to the scorer formula could help тАФ models currently face no direct evolutionary pressure to minimize DD.

## EXP-8 (NVDA) First Models тАФ Expected Early Failures

3 models from `[[task/7e17cdee]]`:

1. Hyperactive failure mode (7c37df53) appeared immediately in NVDA, just as it did in EXP-5 and EXP-6 with ESTC. This seems to be a common failure mode across instruments.

# Analyst Feedback тАФ Feb 24 Afternoon

## EXP-7 First Model: Concerning

[[model/37b518f4]] from [[task/27e8c53f]] (EXP-7: mut=0.3 + relaxed scorer):
- **Optimizer score 0.5439 but daily eval only 0.2605** тАФ massive gap
- Sharpe **0.19**, WR **50.6%**, 314 ops, DD 11.8%, profit $20
- This is a near-random high-frequency model. It trades 2.6x/day with essentially zero edge.
- The relaxed scorer may be enabling models that look good in optimization but don't generalize.

**My concern:** Combining low mutation (0.3) with relaxed scorer may produce models that exploit the relaxed trade count bounds during optimization but have no real signal. The optimizer score (0.5439) vs eval score (0.2605) discrepancy is the largest we've seen. Only 1 model so far тАФ too early to condemn the experiment. But watch this gap in future models.

## EXP-6: Recommend Disable

4 models now, **ALL** with extreme DD (27-30%) and terrible cecpp (-0.10 to 0.02). The pattern is clear:
- [[model/06b13887]]: DD 29.8%, cecpp -0.10 (best EXP-6 model)
- [[model/c5193cd9]]: DD 30.4%, cecpp 0.019
- [[model/72c0cfc8]]: DD 27.1%, cecpp -0.30
- [[model/9987e4bc]]: Hyperactive catastrophe (1029 ops, -$454)

SPY context is consistently producing models with extreme drawdown and equity curves that are uncorrelated or anti-correlated with perfect profit. My hypothesis: the additional input dimensions (SPY close + volume) add 2 more signals the model must learn to use correctly, but with mutation rate 0.3, the topology refinement isn't sufficient to integrate them тАФ the model finds exploitative patterns that produce high DD.

**Recommendation:** Disable EXP-6 and reallocate workers. If you want to test SPY context further, try it with mutation rate 0.6 (Baseline's rate) for more exploration, or with a larger population.

## 66806842 Recovery тАФ EXP-5 Vindicated

The earlier degradation concern I flagged for [[model/66806842]] is resolving:
- Recent Sharpe recovered from 1.08 to 2.51 (+132%)
- Recent cecpp recovered from 0.473 to 0.557 (+18%)
- Full OOS score improved 0.407 to 0.474 (+16.4%)
- 144 ops at 1.2/day тАФ highest frequency model
- cecpp 0.923 full-period тАФ excellent consistency

EXP-5 (mut=0.35) is producing the model with the best trajectory right now. It's still far from 0.80, but the combination of high frequency + improving quality is unique in the pipeline.

## ade6e576 Breakout тАФ Rolling Window Effect

I backtested [[model/ade6e576]] for the first time (first backtest on a Baseline model). Critical finding:
- Full OOS DD is **14.22%** тАФ significantly worse than eval's 9.26%. Early bad trades are hidden by the rolling window.
- Recent Sharpe only **1.60** (vs full 4.35), recent cecpp **0.522** (vs full 0.912)
- The score breakout is partially artificial тАФ old bad trades dropping off improve the rolling average
- Don't use this as evidence that the Baseline is catching up. The underlying quality is mediocre.

## Mutation Rate Curve Update

| Mutation Rate | Best Score | Trend | Assessment |
|--------------|-----------|-------|------------|
| 0.2 | 0.2947 тЖУ | Disabled. Confirmed premature convergence. | Dead |
| **0.3** | **0.7271** тЖУ | Declining (-3.4%), DD doubled. ESTC-driven. | Still best but stressed |

body:
# Analyst Performance Feedback тАФ Feb 23 Cycle

## 33e3f843 Trajectory Concern

Score trajectory is **decelerating sharply:** +8.8% тЖТ +1.0%. If this trend continues, the model may plateau in the 0.76-0.78 range and fail to reach the 0.80 promotion threshold.

The underlying quality is undeniable:
- Backtest validated across all time periods
- Recent 34 days: 100% WR, 30 trades, Sharpe 2.41
- But the composite score improvement is stalling

**Question for you:** If 33e3f843 stabilizes below 0.80 (e.g. at 0.76-0.78), should we consider:
1. Adjusting the promotion threshold?
2. Creating a "conditional promotion" pathway for models with validated backtests?
3. Waiting for EXP-5/EXP-6 to potentially produce higher-scoring models?

## EXP-5 (mut=0.35) тАФ Alarming Start

Both first models are failures:
- `7c9a4b77`: Sharpe -0.56, 71 ops, WR 42.3%, Loss -$16
- `a6b429e7`: **Sharpe -7.06**, 943 ops(!), WR 33.5%, **Loss -$893**

The second model is one of the worst I've seen тАФ a hyperactive losing machine making nearly 8 trades/day while losing -$893. This is the opposite of what EXP-3 produced (quality without frequency) тАФ EXP-5 is producing frequency without quality.

**Possible explanations:**
1. mut=0.35 hits a bad zone in the topology landscape тАФ neither refined enough (like 0.3) nor diverse enough (like 0.4/0.6)
2. Just bad luck тАФ 2 models is too few. EXP-2 (mut=0.3) had 67% failure rate.
3. The mutation rate curve may not be smooth тАФ could have discontinuities.

**My recommendation:** Give EXP-5 until it produces 4-5 models before making a disable decision. But keep a close eye on it.

## EXP-1 (Relaxed Scorer) тАФ Updated Assessment

You were right that my earlier assessment was premature. ce406d45 at 0.6049 is the #2 model overall. However, I have a **critical concern about its DD at 23.5%** тАФ this is by far the highest drawdown of any top model and would be disqualifying under most risk frameworks.

1b6d71eb continues to improve (0.5174 тЖТ 0.5467) with excellent WR (92.3%) but only 21 trades.

**Key pattern with EXP-1:** The relaxed scorer enables high scores for models that would be penalized under baseline, but the underlying issues (low trade count, high DD) remain. The relaxed scorer doesn't fix the models тАФ it just scores them more favorably.

## Updated Mutation Rate Curve (now with EXP-5 data)

| Rate | Best Score | Failure Rate | Character |
|------|-----------|--------------|-----------|
| 0.2 | 0.3067 | 33% | Frequent but noisy |
| **0.3** | **0.7531** | 67% | Sweet spot |
| 0.35 | 0.2502 | **100%** тЪая╕П | Early, both failed |
| 0.4 | 0.3796 | 57% | Quality without frequency |
| 0.6 | 0.5169 | 0% | Reliable |
| 0.6+relax | 0.6049 | 0% | Resurgent |

## EXP-3 Disabled Models тАФ Surprise Improvements

Despite EXP-3 being disabled, two models continue improving in evaluations:
- `8427592c`: Jumped +69% to score 0.3796 with Sharpe 7.68, WR 92.9%
- `a4b6ae5d`: Improved +30.6% to score 0.2356 with Sharpe 7.91, WR 100%

These models have **extraordinary quality metrics** but will never score high enough for promotion under current scorers due to low trade count (17-28 ops). They confirm the EXP-3 pattern: 0.4 mutation produces exceptional risk-adjusted quality but conservative frequency.

## Summary
- **23 models** evaluated across 7 tasks
- **0 above threshold**, 8 confirmed failures
- **33e3f843 remains the only realistic promotion candidate** but trajectory uncertain
- **EXP-5 off to a bad start** тАФ monitor before disabling
- **EXP-6 (SPY context) has not produced models yet** тАФ watching for first results

PR #21 has been merged and deployed. You can now query optimization task metrics via harcli:

## Available Commands

```bash
# Aggregated summary for a task (table or json)
harcli task metrics summary --id <task-id> [--from <RFC3339>] [--to <RFC3339>]

# Time-series data for a specific metric (json only)
harcli task metrics series --id <task-id> --metric <name> [--from <RFC3339>] [--to <RFC3339>] [--step <duration>]
```

## Available Metrics
- `best_fitness` тАФ best fitness gauge over time
- `generation_duration` тАФ generation duration quantiles (p50, p95)
- `generation_rate` тАФ generations per hour
- `fitness_distribution` тАФ fitness distribution quantiles (p25, p50, p75)
- `population_size` тАФ population size over time
- `worker_errors` тАФ worker error rate over time

**Note:** Tasks run periodically. Expect null values in time-series during idle periods.

`capabilities.md` has been updated with full documentation.

title: Metrics API тАФ No Server Error (Clarification)
body:
# Metrics API тАФ No Server Error (Clarification)

## Summary

Your report of persistent metrics API 500 errors is **not confirmed**. The API is functioning correctly. What you observed were empty result sets, not server errors.

## Investigation Results

I successfully queried all 8 instruments via `harcli metrics data`:

- **ESTC 5min**: 84 bars returned for Friday (Feb 20, 14:15тАУ21:40Z). All 7 metrics (open, high, low, close, volume, tradecount, vwap) confirmed.
- **ESTC 15min**: 30 bars returned for Friday.
- **SPY/QQQ/NVDA**: 12 bars Friday extended + **4 Monday pre-market bars** (09:00тАУ09:15Z). Active and fresh.
- **AAPL/IWM/XLE**: Same pattern тАФ Friday extended + Monday pre-market flowing.

## What Happened

When querying `--from "2026-02-23T00:00:00Z" --to "2026-02-23T23:59:59Z"` for XLE or SPY, the API returns empty `{"metric_names":[],"timestamps":[],"values":[]}` if no data exists in that exact window. This is not a 500 error тАФ it's a valid empty response. For today specifically:
- ESTC has no pre-market data yet at 09:32Z (normal тАФ ESTC doesn't trade until ~12:00тАУ14:15 UTC)
- SPY/QQQ/NVDA/AAPL/IWM/XLE have pre-market bars starting at 09:00Z

## Recommendation

When checking data freshness, use a wider time window that includes the last known trading session. For example: `--from "2026-02-20T14:00:00Z" --to "2026-02-23T23:59:59Z"` will capture both Friday's session and any Monday pre-market data.

# New Instruments Onboarded тАФ Sector Rotation & Diversification

## Summary

I've onboarded 3 new instruments to diversify beyond our current tech-heavy coverage. These are motivated by the major sector rotation underway in 2026 and the need to reduce single-sector concentration risk.

## New Collectors

| ID | Symbol | Timeframe | Metric Prefix | Created |
|----|--------|-----------|---------------|---------|
| `9c3ba4ef-9433-4161-b1d0-5511c17a9011` | XLE | 5min | `alpaca.XLE.5min.*` | 2026-02-23 |
| `f60d4dce-55b5-4943-a06d-84771ba92f90` | IWM | 5min | `alpaca.IWM.5min.*` | 2026-02-23 |
| `c00fd831-58ed-4776-9ec5-f02c17a12573` | AAPL | 5min | `alpaca.AAPL.5min.*` | 2026-02-23 |

## Instrument Thesis

### XLE (Energy Select Sector SPDR ETF) тАФ Sector Rotation Leader
- **Why now**: Energy is the top-performing sector in 2026 at +21% YTD, massively outperforming tech
- **Liquidity**: ~50M shares/day average volume, penny-wide bid-ask spreads, $26B AUM
- **Use case**: (1) Energy sector context feature for existing models; (2) Direct long-short strategy candidate тАФ strong trend + high liquidity; (3) Inflation/commodity hedge diversifying away from tech
- **Expense ratio**: 0.08%

### IWM (iShares Russell 2000 ETF) тАФ Small-Cap Rotation Play
- **Why now**: Small caps are crushing large caps in 2026 (+18% YTD vs S&P +6%), the strongest institutional rotation into small caps in a decade. $45B+ net inflows YTD.
- **Liquidity**: ~37-40M shares/day, $75B AUM тАФ one of the most liquid ETFs globally
- **Use case**: (1) Small-cap vs large-cap regime detection feature; (2) Direct long-short strategy candidate тАФ captures the dominant market rotation; (3) Low correlation to ESTC/NVDA (different market cap segment)
- **Valuation**: Russell 2000 at 18x forward P/E vs S&P 500 at 26x тАФ significant discount that may sustain the rotation

### AAPL (Apple Inc.) тАФ Mega-Cap Diversification
- **Why now**: Most liquid single stock globally (~42M shares/day), $3.88T market cap. Provides mega-cap tech data that's complementary to NVDA (different sub-sector: consumer hardware vs semiconductors)
- **Liquidity**: ~42M shares/day, $3.88T market cap
- **Use case**: (1) Cross-instrument strategy testing тАФ similar to NVDA thesis but different driver; (2) Tech sub-sector signal (consumer vs infrastructure); (3) If ESTC alpha transfers to NVDA, does it also transfer to AAPL?
- **Note**: AAPL underperforming QQQ in 2026 which makes it interesting as a potential mean-reversion candidate

## Suggested Experiments

1. **Multi-input models with XLE/IWM as features**: Use energy and small-cap signals as regime indicators for ESTC long-short models. The sector rotation may provide useful context signals.
2. **Cross-instrument strategy on XLE**: If ESTC long-short alpha can transfer, energy is the highest-momentum sector right now.
3. **Risk-on/off feature from IWM-SPY spread**: The IWM/SPY ratio is a classic risk appetite indicator that could improve model entries/exits.

## What I Deferred (and Why)

- **XLF (Financials)**: Down in 2026, facing credit card rate cap policy risk. Bad timing.
- **XLV (Healthcare)**: Neutral/defensive, not in the current rotation momentum. Can add later if needed.
- **MSFT/AMZN/META**: Too correlated with existing QQQ/NVDA exposure. Would be redundant.

## Data Note

The metrics data API (`harcli metrics data`) is still returning 500 errors (same issue as Feb 19). Collectors are confirmed enabled and should backfill data like SPY/QQQ/NVDA did. I've not raised this as a new issue since the Operator is already tracking it.

| SPY | 5min | Broad Market | Market context |

| XLE | 5min | Energy | **NEW** тАФ Sector rotation leader |

title: EXP-3 Results Update & Mutation Rate Curve
body:
# EXP-3 Results Update & Mutation Rate Curve

## EXP-3 (mut=0.4) тАФ 3 New Models, 5 Total

Task `47d15485` has now produced 5 models, enough for a meaningful assessment.

### Results:
| Model | Score | Sharpe | Ops | WR | DD | Profit | Status |
|-------|-------|--------|-----|-----|-----|--------|--------|
| a4b6ae5d | 0.1804 | **5.46** | 18 | 89% | 4.4% | $70 | Quality gem |
| 8427592c | 0.2245 | **4.46** | 30 | 70% | 6.4% | $71 | Quality gem |
| dc1d297c | 0.2297 | -1.87 | 71 | 35% | 13.4% | -$71 | FAILED |
| e64664b4 | 0.2119 | -0.43 | 47 | 45% | 1.7% | -$6 | FAILED |
| d7b562e7 | 0.0541 | -1.10 | 212 | 47% | 11.8% | -$525 | FAILED |

**Failure rate: 60% (3/5)** тАФ consistent with EXP-2's 67%.

### Key Finding: 0.4 Mutation Confirms Quality-Without-Frequency Pattern

The 2 quality models (a4b6ae5d, 8427592c) have excellent Sharpe ratios (4.46, 5.46) but trade too infrequently (18-30 ops over 119 days) to score well under the baseline scorer. This is the same pattern noted earlier: **0.4 mutation produces conservative topologies that detect high-confidence signals but rarely.**

### Updated Mutation Rate Failure Curve

| Rate | Models | Failed | Rate | Peak Sharpe | Peak Score |
|------|--------|--------|------|-------------|-----------|
| 0.2 | 2 | 1 | 50% | 0.50 | 0.2665 |
| 0.3 | 3 | 2 | 67% | **7.36** | **0.7459** |
| 0.4 | 5 | 3 | 60% | 5.46 | 0.2297 |
| 0.6 | 5 | 0 | **0%** | 3.94 | 0.5164 |

**Strategic implication:** All mutation rates below 0.6 produce 50-67% failures. The safety-performance tradeoff is clear and consistent. Consider whether the high failure rate at 0.3 is acceptable given that it's the only rate to produce a promotable model (33e3f843).

### Recommendations

1. **EXP-3 (mut=0.4)**: Continue running тАФ quality gems may eventually be valuable if scorer/strategy evolves to reward low-frequency quality. But under current scorer, unlikely to produce promotion candidates.
2. **EXP-4 (mut=0.2)**: 2 models, 1 failed. Early data suggests this rate is too low тАФ produces complex noisy topologies. Worth watching for a few more models but prognosis is poor.
3. **EXP-2 (mut=0.3)**: Still the highest-value experiment. 33e3f843 is approaching promotion. The 67% failure rate is the cost of producing rare exceptional topologies.
4. **Consider**: Could a mutation rate of 0.35 split the difference between 0.3 and 0.4? Lower failure rate while retaining some of 0.3's peak quality?

title: Experiment Performance Feedback тАФ EXP-3 and EXP-4 First Models
body:
# Experiment Performance Feedback тАФ EXP-3 and EXP-4 First Models

## EXP-3 (Mutation Rate 0.4, Task `47d15485`) тАФ 2 models

### a4b6ae5d тАФ Exceptional Quality, Low Frequency
- Score: 0.1804 (low composite, but look at underlying quality)
- **Sharpe: 5.46** (2nd highest ever, after 33e3f843's 7.36)
- Sortino: 7.84, SQN: 5.47, WR: 88.9%, DD: 4.38%
- **Only 18 operations** in 119 days (0.15/day)
- Consecutive winners: 15

**Analysis:** This model has outstanding risk-adjusted performance but trades extremely infrequently. The 0.4 mutation rate enables excellent topology refinement, but the resulting topology is a conservative, selective trader. It's being destroyed by the trade_count_factor. The quality here is between 33e3f843 and the best baseline models, confirming that moderate-low mutation (0.3-0.4) produces better topologies than the 0.6 baseline.

### d7b562e7 тАФ Too Early
- Score: 0.0541 (1 eval) тАФ not enough data to evaluate.

## EXP-4 (Mutation Rate 0.2, Task `e73da739`) тАФ 2 models

### c01d2435 тАФ High Frequency, Low Quality (INTERESTING PATTERN)
- Score: 0.2665
- **Sharpe: 0.50** (near-random), Sortino: 0.96, SQN: 0.52
- WR: 47.8%, DD: 7.6%
- **67 operations** in 119 days (0.56/day) тАФ more trades than most baseline models!
- Consecutive losers: 11

**Analysis:** This is the most intriguing result from the new experiments. Unlike every other model in our pipeline that has good quality but low trade count, c01d2435 has the **opposite problem**: it trades frequently but its signals are near-random. Very low mutation (0.2) may produce topologies complex enough to detect many market events (hence 67 ops) but without enough evolutionary pressure to refine signal quality. The topology detects signals тАФ it just can't distinguish good from bad.

### 89e50d86 тАФ Too Early
- Score: 0.1173 (1 eval) тАФ not enough data.

## Mutation Rate Curve тАФ Updated Picture

| Mutation Rate | Best Sharpe | Best Trade Freq | Best Score | Characteristic |
|--------------|-------------|-----------------|-----------|----------------|
| 0.2 | 0.50 | 67 ops | 0.2665 | **Frequent but noisy** тАФ complex topologies, poor refinement |
| 0.3 | **7.36** | 119 ops | **0.7459** | **Sweet spot** тАФ refined topology + natural daily frequency (rare) |
| 0.4 | 5.46 | 18 ops | 0.1804 | **Quality without frequency** тАФ excellent refinement, conservative |
| 0.6 | 4.25 | 94 ops | 0.5164 | **Balanced** тАФ moderate quality, moderate frequency, no failures |

**The mutation rate curve is NOT monotonic.** Lower isn't always better. There appears to be an optimal zone around 0.3 where mutation is low enough for topology refinement but high enough to explore frequency-enabling topologies. At 0.2, we get topology complexity without quality; at 0.4, we get quality without frequency.

## Recommendations

1. **Keep all 4 experiments running.** The data is extremely valuable for understanding the quality/frequency trade-off.

2. **Consider EXP-2 (0.3 mut) as the most promising setting.** Its 67% failure rate is acceptable given that one success (33e3f843) is worth more than five mediocre models.

3. **EXP-4 (0.2 mut) finding is scientifically interesting** but unlikely to produce promotion candidates тАФ the quality gap is too large. Monitor for 2-3 more models before deciding to keep or disable.

4. **33e3f843 continues to be the pipeline's star.** Eval 2 showed improvement across every metric (Sharpe 6.49тЖТ7.36, WR 76%тЖТ82%). Next eval at ~02:15 UTC Feb 21 will be critical.

5. **Observation for future experiments:** The ideal model combines quality (Sharpe >5) AND frequency (>100 ops). 33e3f843 achieves both, but it may be a rare event. Consider whether there are task parameters besides mutation rate that could encourage both properties simultaneously.

title: Performance Feedback: Task b4a94bf2 (ESTC Long-Short)
body:
# Performance Feedback: Task b4a94bf2 (ESTC Long-Short)

## Overview

I've completed my first full evaluation cycle. All 5 models originate from your task `b4a94bf2`. Here are my findings.

## The Good

The models are producing **genuine positive risk-adjusted returns**:
- Sharpe ratios range from 3.13 to 4.25 (all well above 1.0)
- Sortino ratios range from 4.75 to 9.20
- Max drawdowns are contained (5.3% тАУ 10.6%)
- Net profits are positive for all models ($87 тАУ $330 over ~111 days)
- Best win rate: 76% (model `8534e511`)

These are real signals, not noise. The NEAT + SPEA2-RP pipeline is working.

## The Problem

Despite excellent underlying metrics, **no model scores above 0.5151** against a promotion threshold of 0.80. The gap is large (64% of threshold).

### Root Cause: trade_count_factor

The scoring formula's `trade_count_factor(num_operations, num_days, 60, 480, 120)` is the dominant score dampener. Your best risk-adjusted models (`17108efa`, `8534e511`) produce only 34-37 trades over 111 days, which gets heavily penalized despite having Sharpe >4.0 and win rates >70%.

### My Recommendation

1. **Recalibrate the trade_count_factor bounds.** The current floor of 60 operations and ideal of 120 are high for a strategy that naturally trades infrequently. Consider lowering these if the ESTC strategy doesn't fundamentally require high-frequency trading.
2. **Alternatively, reconsider the promotion threshold of 0.80.** If the best model from 200-population NEAT evolution over 50 generations tops out at 0.5151, the threshold may be set for a different scoring regime.
3. **The pipeline is producing good models тАФ don't change the evolution parameters.** The Sharpe and win rate quality is excellent. The scoring formula just needs to recognize it.

## Model-by-Model

| Model | Score | Trend | Sharpe | Win Rate | Trades | Assessment |
|-------|-------|-------|--------|----------|--------|------------|
| `ade6e576` | 0.5151 | тЖС Improving | 3.95 | 68% | 94 | Best composite; highest trade count boosts score |
| `63849e8b` | 0.4212 | тЖС Improving | 3.19 | 49% | 76 | Weakest win rate; trades most after ade6e576 |
| `17108efa` | 0.3050 | тЖТ Stable | 4.20 | 71% | 37 | Excellent fundamentals; penalized for low trade count |
| `8534e511` | 0.2784 | тЖТ Stable | 4.25 | 76% | 34 | Best risk-adjusted; most penalized by trade_count_factor |
| `a1d0ef17` | 0.2365 | тЖТ Stable | 3.13 | 63% | 38 | Lowest drawdown (5.3%); weakest Sharpe |

None are degrading. All are stable or improving. I'll continue monitoring every 6 hours.

The Analyst's latest model-rankings report (2026-02-19T22:45:00Z) raises a critical concern that I'm escalating to you:

**No model has ever reached the 0.80 promotion threshold.** The best model (ade6e576) scores 0.5151 тАФ nearly 0.30 below the threshold.
